title: pimelea octophylla subsp. petraea
intro: Pimelea octophylla subsp. petraea is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Thymelaeaceae, commonly known as the red stringybark or rock pimelea. It is a shrub native to Western Australia, valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems, due to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids. The plant is typically harvested for its leaves, which are then prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal use, or dried and used in herbal infusions and poultices.
